title: Incremental Recompute
use_for: turning local source changes into bounded stage reruns
processing change sets instead of broad full-pipeline reruns
designing incremental pipeline updates with explicit retained products
combining invalidation rules with execution budgets
keeping recompute cost proportional to actual change
do_not_use_for: basic stage splitting with no retained state
rules: model change as explicit input to the pipeline, not as hidden side effect
rerun only the stages whose owned products became invalid
bound incremental work when change volume can spike
keep full rerun as an explicit fallback path
prefer stable retained products over ad hoc patching of downstream state
procedure: Define the change-set shape entering the pipeline.
Map which retained products each change kind invalidates.
Define the minimal stage rerun path per change kind.
Add bounded execution or batching if change volume can spike.
Keep a full rerun path for structural or global changes.

primary_when: the request is specifically about incremental updates, change sets, or bounded partial reruns through a pipeline
the request is about making recompute scale with actual change volume
must_not_own_when: the request is mainly about initial stage design with no incremental path
the request is about generic scheduler mechanics without pipeline products

solves: how to process local changes without rerunning the whole pipeline
how to combine retained products, invalidation rules, and bounded execution into one incremental model
how to preserve a correct fallback path when local incremental assumptions break
does_not_solve: basic ownership of source versus derived state by itself

core_principles: Incremental recompute should be driven by explicit change sets and retained products, not hidden patching.
Local reruns are only valid when stage ownership and invalidation paths are already explicit.
A full rerun path should stay available for structural or global changes.
constraints: Incremental bookkeeping must remain cheaper than the broad recompute it replaces.
Partial reruns must preserve correctness under reordering, batching, or overflow.
Structural changes must be able to bypass the incremental path cleanly.
decision_rules: Use incremental-recompute as the owner when the main architectural question is how local changes should flow through retained products and bounded reruns.
If change volume can spike, attach work-budgeting or scheduler support before assuming the incremental path is automatically stable.
If invalidation cannot explain exactly which products become dirty, the design is not ready for incremental recompute.
If structural changes invalidate too much of the graph, keep a deliberate full-rerun path instead of overcomplicating local patch rules.

preferred_patterns: explicit change sets entering the pipeline
bounded partial reruns with retained outputs
avoid_patterns: ad hoc downstream patching with no source-of-truth change model
incremental logic that silently mutates broad shared state
partial rerun paths with no worst-case budget or fallback
treating every change as if it were local when structural impact is broad

recipes.name: Build a bounded incremental pipeline path
recipes.when: Use when a pipeline already has retained products and broad reruns are too costly for common local changes.
steps: Map change kinds to invalidated products and stage rerun paths.
Run only the affected stages and stop at clean boundaries.
Add batching or budget limits if incremental bursts can still spike.
Route structural changes to a deliberate full-rerun path.

pitfalls: Adding incremental complexity before stage ownership and invalidation are explicit.
Assuming local change volume is always small and stable.
Patching downstream products directly until source-versus-derived ownership becomes unclear.
review_checklist: Can each change kind map to a minimal rerun path?
Is incremental bookkeeping cheaper than broad recompute?
Does a correct full-rerun fallback still exist?

references.title: Local Reference: Incremental Recompute
references.interpretation: Use this first for change-set driven reruns, bounded incremental execution, and full-rerun fallback design.
references.why: Use as the primary local guide for practical incremental pipeline architecture.

references.title: Local Reference: Invalidation Propagation
references.interpretation: Use this to align incremental reruns with explicit dirty propagation rules and clean boundaries.
references.why: Use to connect local rerun design with existing invalidation semantics.
